p.strong: Every pointer chase = potential cache miss

g.text: Each node allocated separately at unpredictable addresses
Bucket array access | L1/L2 hit | ~4-12 cycles
Node α pointer chase | CACHE MISS | ~200-300 cycles
Node β pointer chase | CACHE MISS | ~200-300 cycles
Node γ pointer chase | CACHE MISS | ~200-300 cycles
Node δ pointer chase | CACHE MISS | ~200-300 cycles

div.p: Why this is catastrophic: • Each malloc() places nodes at unpredictable heap addresses • No spatial locality → hardware prefetcher cannot help • Every node->next dereference is a potential cache miss • Same O(1) algorithm, but 50-100x slower due to memory latency Compare to open addressing: Sequential probe through contiguous array = 1 cache miss for entire search
